ab: patisali mi su’ aw?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: ho’ ga.
en: B: Yes, I would love to.

ab: patisali mi su’ aw?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: aṟat payux ka kinnaba’un mu, iya’ aw.
en: B:Don’t, I don’t know much about it.

ab: mak musa’ cu matisal sinang gaben, mahnguw su’ aw?
ch: A:你下午有空嗎？我想去拜訪你可以嗎？
en: A: Are you free this afternoon? I’d like to visit you. Is that okay?

ab: ho’, non misu moṟow kay.
en: B: Okay, I’ll wait for you at home.

ab: patisal ta’ tikiy ci insa’ na Itaṟal cuxan i maki’ su moṟow aw?
ch: A:你明天有空嗎?我想請問你關於泰雅族的傳說故事。
en: A: Are you free tomorrow? I’d like to ask you about the legendary story of [the Atayal]. *[Amis] please change to your respective tribe

ab: aṟat cu pakikiy moṟow cuxan, mamaha cu nanahi’.
ch: B:明天我要去山上，不在家喔。
en: B: I’m going to the mountains tomorrow, so I won’t be home.

ab: pinungan mu kumaṟal ka ci’uli ga, isu’ ka ba’ caṟung ci insa’ na Itaṟal ma, pa’uci haku’ tikiy.
ch: A:我聽說您對泰雅族的傳統習俗非常了解，想要請教您一下。
en: A: I heard that you are very familiar with the traditional customs of the Atayal, I would like to ask you a question.

ab: pa’ucan misu ci kinnuba’un mu’, aṟat sun payux ka kinnaba’an mu uṟi.
ch: B:我所了解的不多，但是我會盡量回答。
en: B: I don’t know much about it, but I will try to answer as best I can.

ab: ho’, eṟi nak.
en: A: Okay, no problem.

ab: piṟa’ ingkaṟalan su’ la?
en: A: How old are you?

ab: ma’iru’ mapgan maspat ka ingkaṟalan mu.
en: B: I’m 98 years old.

ab: se’eneng su’ na wa.
en: A: You’re so young.

ab: wah, nabkis cu kung la.
en: B: No, I’m not! I’m old.

ab: minoh su’ inu’?
en: A: Where are you from?

ab: minnoh cu Maṟosan.
en: B: I’m from Wanda.

ab: Itaṟal ka kanel su’ uṟi?
ch: A:請問您的太太也是泰雅族人嗎?
en: A: Is your wife also Atayal?

ab: aṟat wa, Bunun ka hiya’.
en: B: No, she is a Bunun.

ab: amol ka ke’ ba’un su’ kumaṟal?
en: A: What languages do you speak?

ab: ba’ cu kumaṟal ci ke’ na Itaṟal lu Bunun.
ch: B: 我會說泰雅語和布農語。
en: B: I can speak Atayal and Bunun.

ab: ma’as caṟung moh matisal sinang, pa’uci cu ci gagaṟux na maloṟak aw?
ch: A:今天很高興能訪問您，請您談一下，有關狩獵的禁忌好嗎?
en: A: It’s a pleasure to interview you today, can you talk about the taboo of hunting?

ab: ho’, makaruṟux cu musa’ maloṟak i se’eneng cu hang.
ch: B:好的。我年輕的時候很常上山打獵。
en: B: Yes. I used to go hunting in the mountains when I was young.

ab: talagay kinramasan ka pissaba’ su’ soni’, bahbah su’ caṟung.
ch: A:謝謝，您今天所教導的真是太棒了。
en: A: Thank you, what you taught me today was really great.

ab: cincaṟung ka ke’ mu hani. sinbili’ na nabkis rali’ kay.
ch: B:我所説的都是千真萬確的，祖先所流傳的講述。
en: B: All that I say is true, the sayings of my ancestors.

ab: bahbah su’ caṟung, tehuk sani ka pintisalan ta’ soni’.
ch: A:今天的訪談就到這邊。非常謝謝您的分享。
en: A: That’s all for today’s interview. Thank you very much for sharing with us.

ab: patisal ta babuw ni la, bahbah su caṟung.
en: B: You’re welcome, let’s talk again.

ab: mamaha cu hang, mamoh cu mung ci ka isa’ na nabkis rali’ sinang babuw ni luṟi.
ch: A:我要離開了，期待下次聽到更多的故事。
en: A: I’m going to leave now and look forward to hearing more stories next time.

ab: ho’, kinhayuw musa’.
en: B: Okay! Please take care.
